title: Spec-update PR landed: 27 ams.covenant.dev references replaced with the dual-host pattern across 9 files
content: Executes the H artifact from journal/2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv. Replaced 27 ams.covenant.dev occurrences (planning entry estimated 18 by initial grep; actual count was higher because some files held multiple occurrences in code blocks) across SPEC.md, POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, PROTOCOL.md, AMS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ESSAY.md, README.md, and canon/decisions/D0010-observability-via-subscriber-not-wire.md. Substitution conventions: example magic links and curl examples in the klappy namespace use ams.klappy.dev; the SPEC §6 architecture diagram and POC-INFRA §4 topology box label both hosts (ams.klappy.dev, ams.truthkit.ai); SPEC §6 IN-scope row, §9 reversibility table, §11 open decisions (DNS subdomain default + MCP discovery URL) all updated to describe the multi-host CNAME pattern with cross-references to D0011; POC-INFRA §8 wrangler.toml routes block declares both custom-domain entries; AMS_HOST static var dropped (host is request-derived per D0011); §11 smoke-test §13 done-when both updated to verify host parity; SPEC §14 revisions table gained a new v1.0 row dated 2026-05-02 documenting the change. README also updated truthkit.dev → truthkit.ai in the same pass per D0011's truthkit vertical surface decision.

title: One ams.covenant.dev reference intentionally retained in SPEC §14 revisions table
content: The §14 revisions table entry describing the change must name what it replaced. That single occurrence (SPEC.md:258) is the historical record and stays. Same applies to the planning TSV (journal/2026-05-02-ams-poc-build-planning.tsv) which records the prior state. Verification grep across the repo confirms no other deploy-host references remain. The README "Built under Covenant Venture Studio" / covenant.dev reference is correct (org-of-record, not deploy host) and stays.

title: After PR merge, POC-PLAN Day 1 (Worker scaffold + accounts + conversations endpoints) is unblocked
content: The locking surface (SPEC.md) and deeper docs are now consistent on the dual-host deployment per D0007 (spec-as-locking-surface) and SPEC §14 (revision discipline). The next executable step per POC-PLAN.md is Day 1: Worker scaffold under worker/, with src/index.ts routing /v1/accounts and /v1/{namespace}/conversations to handlers, the wrangler.toml from POC-INFRA §8.1 (now declaring both custom-domain routes), and the ConversationDO + SessionDO class skeletons. No code lands until this PR merges. Branch: claude/ams-poc-build-YDTQ0.
